project.name: Pura
project.summary: Pura is a TypeScript immutable-update and persistent-data-structure library that returns native JavaScript types while providing fast immutable operations, Immer-compatible migration paths, produceFast, benchmarks, docs, and an npm package under @sylphx/pura.
goals: Provide a fast, type-safe immutable update library for native Array, Object, Map, and Set values.
Own the @sylphx/pura package, public APIs, persistent data-structure implementation, docs, tests, and benchmarks.
Keep benchmark and compatibility claims reproducible.
nonGoals: Own downstream application state models or product-specific update semantics.
Make performance claims without reproducible benchmark context.
Publish package changes without Changesets intent, CI, and npm readback.

owns.name: pura-package-api
owns.description: Public @sylphx/pura API, native-type return contract, type definitions, and package metadata.

owns.name: persistent-data-structures
owns.description: Immutable update implementation, structural sharing, HAMT/RRB-style internals, tests, docs, and benchmarks.

doesNotOwn: Downstream application state models, UI framework behavior, storage layers, or product-specific update semantics.
Third-party benchmark environments outside documented benchmark scripts and results.
Enterprise doctrine or organization-wide package release policy.
forbiddenCouplings: Do not encode downstream product state semantics into the shared library core.
Do not publish benchmark claims without reproducible benchmark context.
Do not publish package changes from a human shell or personal token.

delivery.deployPath: No hosted deploy path is declared. Package release runs from main through .github/workflows/release.yml using Changesets.
delivery.productionProof: Test workflow, build/test/lint/typecheck evidence, benchmark evidence for performance claims, Changesets release intent, and npm readback for @sylphx/pura.

packageRelease.versionPr: changesets/action creates version changes or publishes from main.
packageRelease.publishProof: npm package readback for @sylphx/pura plus changelog/version evidence.

commercial.metricsSourceOfTruth: benchmark reports and npm package readback
guardrails: Commercial positioning must be backed by benchmark and competitor evidence.
Package claims must not exceed reproducible tests and benchmarks.

gaps.description: Package publication proof should include npm readback and benchmark context when performance claims change.
